title: Houses for rent in Bedford, OH
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Bedford, OH
metaDescription: Searching for houses for rent in Bedford, Ohio? See current Cleveland-area rent ranges, Section 8 voucher facts, and Ohio tenant law, then tell us what you need.
answerBox: Bedford is a Cuyahoga County suburb southeast of Cleveland, next to Bedford Heights along the I-271 corridor. Rent Finder Cleveland's rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland proper and a few nearby Ohio cities, so we don't currently manage property inside Bedford itself — but every home we do manage accepts Housing Choice Vouchers, and we can help you compare nearby options.

sections.h2: Where Bedford, Ohio fits in the Cleveland area
body: Bedford is a Cuyahoga County suburb directly southeast of Cleveland, bordering Bedford Heights and reached from the city via the I-271 corridor. Part of the Cleveland Metroparks' Bedford Reservation, which follows Tinker's Creek, lies within the city, giving the area a mix of residential streets and Metroparks green space.
Housing stock in and around Bedford includes early-to-mid 20th-century single-family homes as well as smaller multi-family duplexes, similar to much of inner-ring Cuyahoga County.

sections.h2: Does Rent Finder Cleveland manage houses for rent in Bedford?
body: Not currently. We manage 90+ rental homes across Greater Cleveland, but that portfolio is concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast neighborhoods — Slavic Village, Collinwood, Glenville, Fairfax/Central, Hough, and Buckeye-Shaker — with a smaller footprint on the west side and a handful of homes in Akron, Lorain, and Elyria, Ohio. We don't have a listing inside Bedford itself as of this writing.

If you're set on Bedford, [tell our leasing team what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) and we'll let you know about comparable Section 8-friendly homes in nearby Cleveland neighborhoods, or point you toward other resources for Bedford specifically. You can also browse our [Cleveland houses pillar page](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) or the full [Cleveland suburbs guide](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/) for other communities where we do have availability.

sections.h2: What do houses rent for near Cleveland's southeast suburbs?
body: There's no Bedford-specific rent index available, but two dated market reports give a useful benchmark for the wider Cleveland metro, which Bedford is part of. Per Zumper's report dated July 4, 2026, the citywide median asking rent is $1,250/month; per RentCafe (Yardi), dated July 2, 2026, the average rent across professionally managed Cleveland apartments is $1,564/month for about 787 square feet. The two differ because RentCafe leans toward larger managed complexes while Zumper reflects the broader listing median, including smaller and older units — both are legitimate reference points.

sections.h2: Section 8 and Housing Choice Vouchers if you're searching near Bedford
body: The Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA) administers Housing Choice Vouchers for all of Cuyahoga County, including Bedford. CMHA accepts preliminary applications year-round with no closing date, through its [Housing Choice Voucher application page](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php); selection then runs by periodic random lottery from that list rather than strict first-come, first-served, so a spot can come up within days or take months. Current CMHA income limits (effective April 3, 2025) are $34,800 for one person, $39,800 for two, $49,700 for four, and $65,650 for eight — always confirm current limits directly with CMHA.

Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept a voucher as a source of income, and Bedford is not among the small set of Cuyahoga County communities reported to have local voucher-protection ordinances (Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale). Confirm a specific landlord's voucher policy directly before applying. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready — read our full [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for how the process works.

sections.h2: Ohio renter protections that apply wherever you rent
body: Ohio's landlord-tenant statute applies statewide, including Bedford. Under [ORC 5321.16](https://codes.ohio.gov/ohio-revised-code/section-5321.16), a security deposit exceeding $50 or one month's rent (whichever is greater) earns 5% annual interest if a tenant stays six months or more, and a landlord must return the deposit with an itemized statement of any deductions within 30 days of move-out. Before filing an eviction for nonpayment, Ohio law requires a landlord to serve a statutory 3-day notice under [ORC 1923.04](https://codes.ohio.gov/ohio-revised-code/section-1923.04). This is general information, not legal advice — verify current statute language before relying on it.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland have houses for rent in Bedford, Ohio right now?
faq.a: Not currently. Our 90+ rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast neighborhoods, plus a few homes in Akron, Lorain, and Elyria. We don't have a Bedford listing today, but you can tell us what you're looking for and we'll flag comparable Section 8-friendly options nearby.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house near Bedford, Ohio?
faq.a: There's no Bedford-specific index, but the wider Cleveland metro's median asking rent was $1,250/month per Zumper (July 4, 2026), while RentCafe's average across managed apartments was $1,564/month (July 2, 2026). Actual Bedford-area rents will vary by home size and condition.

faq.q: Is Section 8 accepted in Bedford, Ohio?
faq.a: Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers, and Bedford is not reported among the handful of Cuyahoga County suburbs with a local voucher-protection ordinance. Acceptance depends on the individual landlord, so confirm directly before applying. CMHA administers vouchers countywide, including Bedford.

faq.q: How do I apply for a Housing Choice Voucher near Bedford?
faq.a: Apply through the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority's (CMHA) online Housing Choice Voucher application, which accepts preliminary applications year-round. Selection runs by periodic random lottery rather than strict order, so wait times vary. Confirm current income limits and required documents directly with CMHA before applying.

faq.q: What Ohio law governs a security deposit for a Bedford rental?
faq.a: Ohio Revised Code 5321.16 applies statewide, including Bedford. Deposits over $50 or one month's rent earn 5% annual interest after six months of tenancy, and landlords must return deposits with an itemized statement within 30 days of move-out. This is general information, not legal advice.
